description_long: This is a historical field cannon, featuring a long, black cast iron barrel mounted on a wooden carriage with two large spoked wheels. The barrel is cylindrical with a slightly flared muzzle and reinforced rings along its length, designed for firing heavy projectiles. The carriage is constructed from sturdy wood, with metal fittings and a split trail at the rear for stability and aiming. The wheels are large, wooden, and spoked, allowing the cannon to be moved across rough terrain. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with visible wear and muted colors, suggesting use in outdoor or combat environments. There are no visible modern features, markings, or camouflage, emphasizing its historical and functional design.
description: A historical field cannon with a long black barrel mounted on a wooden carriage and large spoked wheels. The design is utilitarian, with visible wood and metal construction and no modern features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the black barrel, wooden carriage, and both large spoked wheels.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular muzzle of the barrel and the alignment of the two large wheels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other side of the barrel and carriage.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the split trail of the carriage and the back ends of the wheels and barrel.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the carriage.
